title: 🤖 AI Daily | 2026-05-31
headline: Anthropic launches Cowork, a no-code Claude Desktop agent; Google redesigns search box for first time in 25 years; SoftBank pledges €75B for French data centers.

Product Launches & Updates.title: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
Product Launches & Updates.summary: Anthropic released Cowork, extending Claude Code's power to non-technical users. The team built the feature in about a week and a half, largely using Claude Code itself.

Product Launches & Updates.title: Google just redesigned the search box for the first time in 25 years
Product Launches & Updates.summary: At I/O, Google announced a new search interface that moves beyond the classic white rectangle and blue links, integrating AI-generated answers and multimodal input.

Product Launches & Updates.title: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent
Product Launches & Updates.summary: Salesforce launched a rebuilt Slackbot, transforming it from a notification tool into a full AI agent that can search enterprise data, draft documents, and take actions.

Product Launches & Updates.title: Meta is reportedly developing an AI pendant
Product Launches & Updates.summary: Meta is making big bets on AI-powered hardware, with a reported AI pendant in development.

Product Launches & Updates.title: GitHub Copilot's new token-based billing spurs developer backlash
Product Launches & Updates.summary: Developers are upset over GitHub Copilot's switch to token-based billing, with many calling it a 'joke' and signaling the end of the golden age of Copilot.

Industry Moves & Funding.title: SoftBank says it will invest up to €75 billion to build French data centers
Industry Moves & Funding.summary: SoftBank plans to develop and operate up to 5 GW of additional data center capacity in France, a massive bet on AI infrastructure.

Industry Moves & Funding.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
Industry Moves & Funding.summary: Railway raised $100M Series B to build an AI-native cloud platform, having amassed 2 million developers without marketing spend.

Industry Moves & Funding.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt
Industry Moves & Funding.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M to scale AI customer interviews, following a viral $5,000 billboard that helped hire over 100 engineers.

Industry Moves & Funding.title: Snap alums unveil Ghost Angels fund
Industry Moves & Funding.summary: 20 Snap alumni launched Ghost Angels, a fund to back the next generation of social media startups.

Industry Moves & Funding.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month — Goose does the same thing for free
Industry Moves & Funding.summary: Open-source alternative Goose offers similar AI coding agent capabilities to Claude Code, but at no cost, challenging Anthropic's pricing.

Research & Papers.title: Nous Research's NousCoder-14B matches larger proprietary coding models
Research & Papers.summary: Nous Research released NousCoder-14B, an open-source coding model trained in just four days on 48 Nvidia B200 GPUs, matching or exceeding several larger proprietary systems.

Research & Papers.title: How a new extraction process could unlock the world's lithium
Research & Papers.summary: Researchers published a new method for extracting lithium from rock that could be more environmentally friendly and cheaper than existing techniques.

Opinion & Deep Dives.title: The groupthink boom: what three top VCs really think about the AI frenzy
Opinion & Deep Dives.summary: VCs discuss the AI investment frenzy, noting that young founders in AI are receiving term sheets easily, with some humor about the ease of funding.

Opinion & Deep Dives.title: How the Pope's Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment
Opinion & Deep Dives.summary: Pope Leo XIV's encyclical on AI argues 'Technology is never neutral' and calls for courage and solidarity in the AI era.

Opinion & Deep Dives.title: The AI Hype Index: AI gets booed in graduation season
Opinion & Deep Dives.summary: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt was booed by University of Arizona graduates when he urged them to help shape AI, reflecting public skepticism.
